operating_state: Net sales declined 17% to $13.2 billion in Q3 2024 driven by lower shipment volumes across all segments amid declining commodity prices and elevated interest rates. Nine-month sales fell 11% to $40.6 billion with production and precision agriculture declining broadly across global markets, while construction and forestry decreased due to moderating U.S. demand and planned underproduction to retail levels. Financial services revenues increased 21% in Q3 driven by higher average portfolio balances despite margin compression from elevated funding costs.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio increased to 68.9% from 67.4% prior year as fixed overhead costs created production inefficiencies from reduced volumes, partially offset by price realization and lower material costs. Operating margins compressed across all segments with production and precision agriculture falling to 22.8% from 26.2%. Research and development expenses increased 7% reflecting continued technology investments, while interest expense surged to $840 million from $623 million due to higher debt levels and rates.
material_events: The company repurchased $3.2 billion of common stock during nine months, reducing share count to 274.5 million from 290.8 million prior year. Board authorized sale of 50% ownership in Banco John Deere to Banco Bradesco, expected to close in Q2 2025 with assets classified as held for sale. Implemented employee-separation programs for salaried workforce with pretax expenses recorded. Quarterly dividend increased to $1.47 per share from $1.25 per share prior year.
macro_exposures: Elevated interest rates continue impacting customer borrowing costs and demand while compressing financial services financing spreads. Global agricultural fundamentals moderating due to rising grain stocks, lower commodity prices, and geopolitical uncertainty affecting farmer income margins. Construction industry facing headwinds from decreased housing starts and low commercial real estate construction. Currency translation adjustments resulted in negative impact for nine months with significant exposure through Euro-denominated debt.
forward_outlook: Production volumes expected to continue declining through remainder of 2024 due to reduced demand and planned underproduction strategies. Large and small agricultural equipment sales forecasted down from 2023 levels across all major markets, while construction equipment industry sales expected down with infrastructure spending benefits offset by housing and commercial real estate weakness. Financial services net income expected up moderately driven by higher average portfolio balances, partially offset by provision for credit losses.
uncertainty_profile: Credit quality deterioration in financing receivables presents ongoing risk with credit loss provisions increasing to $229 million for nine months from $75 million prior year, reflecting elevated delinquencies and declining market conditions. The Brazil financial services divestiture remains pending regulatory approval with valuation allowances recorded against held-for-sale assets. Subject to various unresolved legal actions and provides guarantees to banks for retail financing in certain international markets.
